rulings.Case Title: LEONARD SHIELDS et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Ntc Of Mo & Mo To Consolidate Actions For All Purposes
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JANUARY 7, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 1, Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.¿s motion to consolidate actions for all purposes is granted. No opposition filed. Cases numbered 274740 and 276124 are consolidated. Henceforth all papers shall be filed under the lead case no. 274740. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ARVIN REECE, ET AL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Apply Idaho Law Regarding: (1) Limitations On Plaintiffs' Recoverable Non-Economic Damages; And (2) Plaintiffs, Product Liability And Design Defect Claims
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JANUARY 7, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 2, Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.¿s motion to apply Idaho law regarding (1) limitations on non-economic damages and (2) design defect claims is denied without prejudice. Request for such relief needs to be brought either as part of a substantive motion where the Court¿s determination of which state¿s law to apply would affect its determination on the merits of the motion, or before the trial judge. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JACK SOWERS et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JANUARY 7, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 3, Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.¿s general demurrer to plaintiffs¿ first amended complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. Defendant¿s request to order plaintiffs to identify individuals with knowledge regarding defendant¿s liability is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT SOTELO SR VS. CALAVERAS ASBESTOS, LTD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel-Civil
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JANUARY 7, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 7, Brayton Purcell¿s motion to be relieved as counsel for Robert Sotelo, Jr. is granted. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless Brayton Purcell notifies plaintiff of this tentative ruling and he states that he does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Brayton Purcell must bring a proposed order to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. rulings.Case Title: LOUIS BLAZEJEWSKI VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JANUARY 7, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 4, Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, plaintiff¿s evidence is insufficient to create a triable issue whether the gaskets he removed from Crane Co. valves aboard the Thetis Bay were original components. The court sustains defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration about removing packing, about the Caloosahatchee ship and about using Cranite as contradictory to his deposition testimony. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: SANDY MCINISH, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR IN et al VS. ASBESTOS COMPANIES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of The Strict Liability Cause Of Action And The Claim For Punitive Damages
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JANUARY 7, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 5, Defendant Hopeman Brothers, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment or in the alternative summary adjudication is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)
